root_text.an5.170:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ānando kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme.
root_text.an5.170:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā bhaddaji yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā ānandena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an5.170:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ bhaddajiṁ āyasmā ānando etadavoca:
root_text.an5.170:1.4: “kiṁ nu kho, āvuso bhaddaji, dassanānaṁ aggaṁ, kiṁ savanānaṁ aggaṁ, kiṁ sukhānaṁ aggaṁ, kiṁ saññānaṁ aggaṁ, kiṁ bhavānaṁ aggan”ti?
root_text.an5.170:2.1: “Atthāvuso, brahmā abhibhū anabhibhūto aññadatthudaso vasavattī, yo taṁ brahmānaṁ passati, idaṁ dassanānaṁ aggaṁ.
root_text.an5.170:2.2: Atthāvuso, ābhassarā nāma devā sukhena abhisannā parisannā.
root_text.an5.170:2.5: Yo taṁ saddaṁ suṇāti, idaṁ savanānaṁ aggaṁ.
root_text.an5.170:2.7: Te santaṁyeva tusitā sukhaṁ paṭivedenti, idaṁ sukhānaṁ aggaṁ.
root_text.an5.170:2.8: Atthāvuso, ākiñcaññāyatanūpagā devā, idaṁ saññānaṁ aggaṁ.
root_text.an5.170:2.9: Atthāvuso, nevasaññānāsaññāyatanūpagā devā, idaṁ bhavānaṁ aggan”ti.
root_text.an5.170:2.10: “Sameti kho idaṁ āyasmato bhaddajissa, yadidaṁ bahunā janenā”ti?
root_text.an5.170:3.3: “Tenahāvuso bhaddaji, suṇāhi, sādhukaṁ manasi karohi; bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an5.170:3.4: “Evamāvuso”ti kho āyasmā bhaddaji āyasmato ānandassa paccassosi.
root_text.an5.170:4.1: “Yathā passato kho, āvuso, anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hoti, idaṁ dassanānaṁ aggaṁ.
root_text.an5.170:4.2: Yathā suṇato anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hoti, idaṁ savanānaṁ aggaṁ.
root_text.an5.170:4.3: Yathā sukhitassa anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hoti, idaṁ sukhānaṁ aggaṁ.
root_text.an5.170:4.4: Yathā saññissa anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hoti, idaṁ saññānaṁ aggaṁ.
root_text.an5.170:4.5: Yathā bhūtassa anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hoti, idaṁ bhavānaṁ aggan”ti.

translation_text.an5.170:1.1: At one time Venerable Ānanda was staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery.
translation_text.an5.170:1.2: Then Venerable Bhaddaji went up to Venerable Ānanda, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an5.170:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side, and Venerable Ānanda said to him:
translation_text.an5.170:1.4: “Reverend Bhaddaji, what is the best sight, the best sound, the best happiness, the best perception, and the best state of existence?”
translation_text.an5.170:2.1: “Reverend, there is this Divinity, the vanquisher, the unvanquished, the universal seer, the wielder of power. When you see the Divinity, that’s the best sight.
translation_text.an5.170:2.2: There are the gods called ‘of streaming radiance’, who are drenched and steeped in pleasure.
translation_text.an5.170:2.3: Every so often they feel inspired to exclaim:
translation_text.an5.170:2.5: When you hear that, it’s the best sound.
translation_text.an5.170:2.6: There are the gods called ‘of universal beauty’.
translation_text.an5.170:2.7: Since they’re truly content, they experience pleasure. This is the best happiness.
translation_text.an5.170:2.8: There are the gods reborn in the dimension of nothingness. This is the best perception.
translation_text.an5.170:2.9: There are the gods reborn in the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception. This is the best state of existence.”
translation_text.an5.170:2.10: “So, Venerable Bhaddaji, do you agree with what most people say about this?”
translation_text.an5.170:3.2: Why don’t you clarify this yourself?”
translation_text.an5.170:3.3: “Well then, Reverend Bhaddaji, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an5.170:4.1: “What you see when the defilements are ended without delay is the best sight.
translation_text.an5.170:4.2: What you hear when the defilements are ended without delay is the best sound.
translation_text.an5.170:4.3: The happiness you feel when the defilements are ended without delay is the best happiness.
translation_text.an5.170:4.4: What you perceive when the defilements are ended without delay is the best perception.
translation_text.an5.170:4.5: The state of existence in which the defilements are ended without delay is the best state of existence.”
